title: Live Well, Age Well Programme
text:
Live Well, Age Well Programme - SupportGoWhere
Live Well, Age Well Programme
Share link
The Live Well, Age Well programme is co-organised by HPB and PA to provide holistic activities to improve the physical, mental and social health of adults aged 50 and above.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Discover free activities near you to stay healthy and active
Download the Healthy 365 mobile app on Apple Store / Google Play
Collapse all sections
About this support

As we age, staying healthy and active is important so you can continue doing the activities you love.

The Live Well, Age Well programme features a wide range of FREE^ exciting activities in your neighbourhood to help you kickstart your healthy living journey.

Activities are specially designed based on the needs of adults aged 50 years old and above, so as to help you improve your physical, mental and social health to continue doing what you love.

These programmes are available at multiple locations near you, including Community Centres/Clubs, Residents’ Committee, Residents’ Network Centres, Senior Activity Centres. Some programmes (e.g. group exercise sessions and workshops) are also available online, so you can stay healthy in the comfort of your home.

^Except for subsidised health screening programmes under Live Assured.

Who is this for?

All Singapore residents aged 50 and above are welcome to join our Live Well, Age Well programme.

There may be additional criteria for selected programmes.

Visit Live Well, Age Well website for more information.

How to apply?

Step 1: Download the Healthy 365 mobile app on Apple Store / Google Play.

Step 2: Create profile if you are a new user. Log in to your existing profile with your NRIC and registered phone number if you are an existing user.

Step 3: Tap on "Explore" and select "Events" to view upcoming events.

Step 4: Search "Active Ageing" to filter for Live Well, Age Well programmes.

Step 5: Select the event you are interested in to view more details. Select "Book Now" to sign up.

You may also visit your nearest Community Clubs/Centres (CCs) or Residents’ Committees (RC)/Residents’ Network (RN) Centres for assistance.

Where can I find help?

View more details

HPB hotline: 1800 223 1313

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
